codeReview.summary: The fix commit adequately resolves all three previously blocking issues: ZIP context false boosts from lowercase words, position-only text-index dedup semantics, and sequential redactDocument canvas release via incremental jsPDF assembly. Regression coverage was added for each fix area. One previously known non-blocking text-index empty-item separator issue remains in this commit.

issues.description: When the previous sorted item has an empty string, indexPage still uses that empty item for EOL/y-gap/gap calculations, which can insert synthetic separators that shift normalized offsets. This was previously identified as non-blocking and is not addressed in this fix commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture note for text indexing still says duplicate/overlapping items are handled but does not define dedup key semantics, which previously allowed a position+text implementation to pass local reasoning but diverge from contract intent.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:201 mentions duplicate/overlapping handling generically; the required position-only key had to be specified in mission feature text and was corrected in src/core/text-index.ts (commit c02116d).

summary: PASS: fix-engine-blocking (c02116d) resolves all three prior blocking findings (ZIP context case-sensitivity, text dedup key semantics, and redactDocument sequential memory release). A previously known non-blocking empty-item separator behavior in text-index remains in this commit.
